steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures at the blank
steps.content: The blank separates two independent clauses: the first defines the Nobel Prize criteria (subject: prize, verb: is given), and the second provides a 2004 example (subject: judges, verb: recognized).

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: A semicolon joins two independent clauses within a single sentence, with the closing quotation mark preceding it per American English conventions. Choice D correctly applies this rule, yielding “direction”; in 2004, for instance... The other options either omit a connector (A), create a comma splice (C), or use improper punctuation (B). That matches choice D — D is correct.
